adsorption.energy_interpretation: E_ads < 0 usually indicates exothermic or energetically favorable adsorption under the chosen reference state, while E_ads > 0 suggests weak or unfavorable adsorption. Final interpretation may require ZPE, entropy, solvation, and reference-state corrections.
adsorption.input_sets.no_usable: No Input Sets are currently usable for VASP. Generate or validate an Input Set first.

adsorption.legacy.title: Legacy
adsorption.oszicar_steps_empty: No OSZICAR energy step data has been saved yet.
adsorption.raw_only: Showing raw adsorption energy only; no ZPE, entropy, or solvent corrections are included.
adsorption.result.method_explanation: This result is based on final TOTEN values from VASP OUTCAR files and uses a DFT total-energy difference. Standard PBE/PBE-D3/HSE06/DFT+U workflows should not be described as a Hartree-Fock formula.
adsorption.result.not_ready: One or more OUTCAR final TOTEN values are missing, so adsorption energy cannot be calculated yet.
adsorption.result.physical_meaning_text: E_ads = E(surface + adsorbate) - E(clean surface) - E(isolated adsorbate)
adsorption.result.title: Adsorption Energy Result
adsorption.title: Adsorption Energy
adsorption.visualization.title: Adsorption Energy Visualization

adsorption.warning.missing_metrics: No parsed metrics are available for this job.
adsorption.warning.missing_required_ener: One or more OUTCAR final TOTEN values are missing, so adsorption energy cannot be calculated yet.
adsorption.warning.missing_toten: OUTCAR exists, but final TOTEN was not parsed. The calculation may be incomplete or interrupted.
adsorption.warning.non_static_calculatio: This job may not be a static single-point calculation and is not recommended for final adsorption energy.

adsorption_wizard.not_called_10a: This VASPKIT structure editing step is not called in stage 10A.
adsorption_wizard.run_105: Run VASPKIT 105 to create candidate POSCAR
adsorption_wizard.source_type.already_sl: Flow: CIF → VASPKIT 105 → clean POSCAR, or upload a clean POSCAR directly. Optional 401 supercell and 402 fixed atoms can be applied later.
adsorption_wizard.source_type.bulk_surfa: Recommended first version: upload an already prepared clean slab POSCAR. Automated 803/801 support will be connected later.
adsorption_wizard.source_type.label: Clean structure source type
adsorption_wizard.source_type.porous_or_: Flow: CIF → VASPKIT 105 → clean POSCAR. 803 slab cutting and 801 vacuum construction are usually not used for porous or MOF models.
adsorption_wizard.step_not_implemented_1: This step will be connected in a later phase. Stage 10A only implements Steps 1–3.
adsorption_wizard.structure_edit_real_ca: Stage 10B calls VASPKIT 105/801/803/401/402 for real. Outputs are saved as candidate files first and update the clean POSCAR only after adoption.
adsorption_wizard.structure_edit_require: These structure editing steps require a POSCAR in the current directory. Adopt a clean POSCAR first.
adsorption_wizard.structure_edit_todo_10: In stage 10A, 801/803/401/402 provide forms, parameter validation, and TODO notes only. They do not call VASPKIT.
adsorption_wizard.structure_editor.adopt: Adopt this candidate as clean POSCAR
Candidate file was adopted as the current clean POSCAR.

adsorption_wizard.structure_editor.exper: Experimental: this VASPKIT prompt may differ between versions.
adsorption_wizard.structure_editor.fix_a: Fixed atoms affect slab relaxation results. Carefully verify the fixed layer or atom selection.
adsorption_wizard.structure_editor.inval: Invalid parameters; VASPKIT was not called: {error}
adsorption_wizard.structure_editor.no_cl: No adopted clean POSCAR is available, so 801/803/401/402 cannot run.
adsorption_wizard.structure_editor.slab_: Slab cutting and termination affect adsorption sites and surface chemistry. Inspect the structure manually before adopting it.
adsorption_wizard.title: Adsorption Workflow Wizard
adsorption_wizard.tutorial.molecule_posc: How to build an isolated molecule POSCAR

1. Build the molecule with VESTA, ASE, or a hand-written POSCAR.
2. Use a 15–20 Å box.
3. For isolated molecules such as NH3, later KPOINTS usually should be Gamma-only 1×1×1.
4. Upload the molecule POSCAR.
adsorption_wizard.tutorial.vesta_adsorbe: How to build an adsorbed POSCAR with VESTA

1. Open the clean POSCAR in VESTA.
2. Manually add NH3 or another adsorbate.
3. Adjust adsorption site, height, and orientation.
4. Export as a VASP POSCAR.
5. Upload it to this system.

vaspkit.common_incar_key.help: Select one common VASPKIT INCAR key; advanced combinations can be entered below.

vaspkit.full_input_set_only: This page only generates a complete VASP input set: POSCAR, INCAR, KPOINTS, and POTCAR. The input set becomes usable for real VASP only when all four files are generated successfully.
vaspkit.generation_mode.help: This phase only saves the generation request draft; it does not call real VASPKIT.

vaspkit.input_set_saved_no_legacy_task: The generated files are managed as an Input Set. No legacy task is created and VASP is not started automatically.
vaspkit.option.accuracy_preset.help: Based on VASPKIT warm tips: Gamma-only, Low, Medium, Fine, or Custom.
vaspkit.option.accuracy_preset.label: Accuracy preset
vaspkit.option.custom_element_order.help: Required when custom order mode is selected. Separate element symbols with spaces.
vaspkit.option.element_order_mode.help: Use VASPKIT default element order, or specify a custom order such as O Ce P.
vaspkit.option.element_order_mode.label: Element order mode
vaspkit.option.existing_potcar_policy.he: Choose what to do if POTCAR already exists in run/: skip, backup and regenerate, or fail.
vaspkit.option.incar_custom_key_string.h: Allows combined strings such as STH6D3, SRD3, or STPU; only VASPKIT menu keys may be concatenated.
vaspkit.option.incar_key_parameters.help: Maps to VASPKIT 101 presets such as SR, ST, D3, H6, and others.
vaspkit.option.kmesh_resolved_value.help: In units of 2π/Å; the test record used 0.04, and 0.03-0.04 is generally precise enough.
vaspkit.option.kmesh_scheme.help: Maps to VASPKIT K-Mesh Scheme menu choices 1/2/3.
vaspkit.option.kmesh_scheme.label: K-mesh scheme
vaspkit.option.potcar_mode.help: 103 uses the recommended default potential; 104 is user specified and reserved behind whitelist protection.
vaspkit.option.potcar_mode.label: POTCAR generation mode
vaspkit.option.uploaded_cif.help: The source structure uploaded by the user; the VASPKIT main flow accepts CIF only in this phase.
vaspkit.option.uploaded_cif.label: Uploaded CIF file
vaspkit.potcar_default_103_only: POTCAR will be generated with VASPKIT 103 default recommended potentials. User-specified potential mode 104 is not exposed in the normal UI yet.
vaspkit.potcar_no_full_preview: Safety limit: full POTCAR preview is forbidden; only a summary is shown.
